name: MATHS
description: Analog function generator. Four channels: two full-featured (1 & 4) with rise/fall/curve and cycle, two attenuverter/offset channels (2 & 3) with unity mixing.

controls.name: Curve
controls.detail: CCW: expo · noon: linear · CW: log

controls.name: Attenuverters
controls.detail: CCW: invert · noon: zero · CW: unity

controls.name: Offset
controls.description: DC offset. Adds to SUM output.

inputs.name: CH 1 Rise CV
inputs.description: Linear CV of CH 1 rise time. +V lengthens rise, −V shortens.

inputs.name: CH 1 Fall CV
inputs.description: Linear CV of CH 1 fall time. +V lengthens fall, −V shortens.

inputs.name: CH 1 Both CV
inputs.description: Bi-polar exponential CV over the entire CH 1 function. +V shortens total time, −V lengthens.

inputs.name: CH 1 Cycle In
inputs.description: Gate HIGH cycles CH 1, gate LOW stops cycling (unless Cycle button engaged).

inputs.name: CH 4 Rise CV
inputs.description: Linear CV of CH 4 rise time. +V lengthens rise, −V shortens.

inputs.name: CH 4 Fall CV
inputs.description: Linear CV of CH 4 fall time. +V lengthens fall, −V shortens.

inputs.name: CH 4 Both CV
inputs.description: Bi-polar exponential CV over the entire CH 4 function. +V shortens total time, −V lengthens.

inputs.name: CH 4 Cycle In
inputs.description: Gate HIGH cycles CH 4, gate LOW stops cycling (unless Cycle button engaged).

outputs.name: CH 1 Unity Out
outputs.description: CH 1 function/envelope tapped before the attenuverter. Patching here does NOT remove CH 1 from the SUM/OR bus.

outputs.name: CH 1 EOR
outputs.description: End-of-rise gate from CH 1 only. Goes high at peak of CH 1 envelope.

outputs.name: Variable Out 1
outputs.description: CH 1 after its attenuverter. Normalled to the SUM/OR bus — patching removes CH 1 from SUM/OR.

outputs.name: Variable Out 2 (CH 2 Out)
outputs.description: CH 2 attenuverter output. Normalled to the SUM/OR bus.

outputs.name: Variable Out 3 (CH 3 Out)
outputs.description: CH 3 attenuverter output. Normalled to the SUM/OR bus.

outputs.name: Variable Out 4
outputs.description: CH 4 after its attenuverter. Normalled to the SUM/OR bus — patching removes CH 4 from SUM/OR.

outputs.name: CH 4 Unity Out
outputs.description: CH 4 function/envelope tapped before the attenuverter. Patching here does NOT remove CH 4 from the SUM/OR bus.

outputs.name: CH 4 EOC
outputs.description: End-of-cycle gate from CH 4 only. Goes high at end of CH 4 fall stage.

outputs.name: SUM
outputs.description: Analog sum of the four Variable Outs (post-attenuverter).

outputs.name: INV
outputs.description: Inverted SUM — same shape as SUM, flipped. Useful for reversed modulation when the destination has no invert input.

outputs.name: OR
outputs.description: Analog OR — highest instantaneous voltage across the four Variable Outs. Positive-only; ignores negative voltages.

behaviors.name: Cycle Mode
behaviors.description: Self-triggering LFO. Speed set by Rise + Fall. LED indicates cycle. Syncs to trigger input when patched — rising edge resets the cycle, enabling clock-locked LFOs.

behaviors.name: Envelope
behaviors.description: Patch gate to TRIG for ASR (sustain during gate high, release on gate low). Patch gate to SIGNAL IN for the same behavior with the signal path active. Patch trigger for AD — rise fires on trigger, fall fires immediately at peak.

behaviors.name: Slew Limiter
behaviors.description: Portamento / glide. Patch pitch CV to SIGNAL IN with nothing in TRIG. Rise = glide up speed, Fall = glide down. Set Rise CCW and Fall CW for instant-up, slow-down portamento.

behaviors.name: Retrigger blocking
behaviors.description: MATHS ignores triggers arriving during Rise. Only retriggers during Fall. Enables analog clock division — set Rise time longer than the incoming clock period to skip every other trigger.

behaviors.name: Vari-Response curve
behaviors.description: Full CCW = logarithmic curve (slow start, fast finish — sounds natural on decay). Noon = linear. Full CW = exponential (fast start, slow tail — snappy attack feel). Applies to both Rise and Fall simultaneously; use CH2/3 attenuverters to offset the resulting shape asymmetrically.

behaviors.name: SUM / OR bus normalization
behaviors.description: Patching a cable from CH 1 OUT or CH 4 OUT removes that channel from the SUM and OR buses. You cannot use an individual output AND have it contribute to SUM/OR simultaneously — use a mult or stackable if you need both.

behaviors.name: CH 2 / CH 3 normalization gotcha
behaviors.trigger: CH 2 or CH 3 attenuverter at non-noon position with nothing patched
behaviors.description: CH 2 Signal In normalizes to an internal ±10V reference; CH 3 normalizes to +5V DC. Attenuverters at non-noon with nothing patched inject DC offset into the SUM output. Set both to noon or insert a dummy cable to eliminate bleed.

behaviors.name: Analog OR for envelope priority
behaviors.description: OR OUT always follows whichever of CH 1 or CH 4 is higher at any moment. Two envelopes with different timing on OR OUT produces a combined shape that always takes the longest/loudest — useful for VCA accent without mixing.

behaviors.name: EOC / EOR as clock outputs
behaviors.description: CH 4 EOC fires a gate at the end of every fall — use as clock output or to trigger other modules. CH 1 EOR fires at the peak of its rise. Chaining EOR → CH 4 TRIG and EOC → CH 1 TRIG creates quadrature LFOs (90° phase offset) without Cycle mode.

behaviors.name: BOTH input normalization
behaviors.trigger: patching to Trigger or Signal In
behaviors.description: The TRIG and SIGNAL IN jacks are normalled — a signal patched to TRIG also acts on SIGNAL IN unless SIGNAL IN is separately patched. This means a gate patched to TRIG controls both timing and signal path simultaneously by default.

patchIdeas.name: Bouncing ball (self-accelerating envelope)
patchIdeas.patch: Positive-feedback envelope that speeds up on each cycle, like a ball bouncing and losing energy.
steps: Press CH 1 Cycle so it's lit — the envelope free-runs.
Set the CH 1 attenuverter (center column, '1') to full CW so Variable Out 1 carries the envelope at unity.
Set the CH 2 attenuverter to about 2 o'clock — this knob sets the feedback depth.
Patch CH 1 Variable Out 1 (bottom row) → CH 2 Signal In.
Patch CH 2 Out → CH 1 Fall CV. Each cycle now shortens its own fall.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out (top, big arrow) → your VCA CV to actually hear the envelope.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 Unity Out — accelerating envelope, 0–8V.
patchIdeas.hear: A decay that gets shorter on every repetition until it chatters. Turn CH 2 attenuverter CW for faster acceleration, CCW to slow it down; near noon the feedback stalls and it free-runs steady.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON
* CH 1 Atten: full CW
* CH 2 Atten: ~2 o'clock

- MATHS.CH 1 (Variable Out 1) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Fall CV) // shortens fall each cycle
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) >> VCA (CV) // audible envelope

patchIdeas.name: Quadrature LFOs
patchIdeas.patch: Two LFOs, 90° out of phase, running off each other's end-of-stage pulses — no Cycle button needed.
steps: Leave CH 1 and CH 4 Cycle buttons OFF.
Set CH 1 Rise and Fall to the same time, matching what you want as the overall LFO period.
Set CH 4 Rise and Fall to the same matching time.
Patch CH 1 EOR → CH 4 Trigger.
Patch CH 4 EOC → CH 1 Trigger. The pair now self-sustains.
Briefly press CH 1 Cycle once to kick it off, then release.
Take CH 1 Unity Out → Filter Cutoff (or any CV destination).
Take CH 4 Unity Out → VCA CV (90° ahead of CH 1).
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 Unity Out and CH 4 Unity Out — two 0–8V envelopes, 90° apart.
patchIdeas.hear: Two modulation sources that feel related but never land at the same place at the same time — filter and VCA breathe against each other for animated timbral motion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle OFF, Rise + Fall matched
* CH 4: Cycle OFF, Rise + Fall matched

- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- MATHS.CH 4 (Unity Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: ASR envelope with sustain plateau
patchIdeas.patch: Trick MATHS into acting like an attack-sustain-release envelope by patching the gate into Signal In instead of Trigger.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise to your desired attack time.
Set CH 1 Fall to your desired release time.
Patch your gate source → CH 1 Signal In (NOT Trigger).
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → VCA CV.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 Unity Out — envelope that attacks, holds, then releases, 0–8V.
patchIdeas.hear: While the gate is high, the envelope reaches full level and stays there (the plateau). When the gate drops, it falls at the Fall rate. Using Trigger instead gives AD only — no sustain.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise = attack, Fall = release

- Gate Source (Gate) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In) // not Trig — gives true ASR
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Push-pull crossfade
patchIdeas.patch: One MATHS envelope simultaneously opens one VCA while closing another — crossfades between two sound sources with a single modulator.
steps: Press CH 1 Cycle and set Rise + Fall for a slow sweep (several seconds).
Set CH 2 attenuverter full CCW — this inverts whatever enters CH 2 Signal In.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → VCA 1 CV. (Unity Out stays available; patching it does not remove CH 1 from the SUM bus.)
Patch CH 1 Variable Out 1 → CH 2 Signal In. Now CH 2 holds an inverted copy of the CH 1 envelope.
Patch CH 2 Out → VCA 2 CV.
Patch sound source A → VCA 1, sound source B → VCA 2, and mix their outputs.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA 1 Out + VCA 2 Out summed at a mixer — crossfaded audio.
patchIdeas.hear: As the envelope rises, VCA 1 opens and VCA 2 closes; as it falls, the reverse. Continuous movement between the two sources instead of a hard cut.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, slow rate
* CH 1 Atten: full CW
* CH 2 Atten: full CCW (invert)

- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) >> VCA 1 (CV) // opens
- MATHS.CH 1 (Variable Out 1) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> VCA 2 (CV) // inverted — closes
- VCA 1 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- VCA 2 (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Envelope follower
patchIdeas.patch: Turn MATHS into an envelope follower — pull a smooth CV that tracks the amplitude of any incoming audio.
steps: Set CH 4 Rise at noon.
Set CH 4 Fall past noon CW for gentle tracking, or CCW for tighter faster tracking.
Patch your audio source (voice, drum loop, whatever) → CH 4 Signal In.
Patch OR Out → your CV destination (Filter Cutoff is the classic target).
patchIdeas.signalOut: OR Out — positive-only CV that rises and falls with audio amplitude.
patchIdeas.hear: Auto-wah behavior: the filter opens on loud transients and closes on quiet passages. Fall knob is your release — turn it CW to smooth out quick transients, CCW to make it snappy.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Rise noon, Fall past noon CW

- Audio Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 4 (Signal In)
- MATHS (OR) >> Filter (Cutoff) // auto-wah envelope

patchIdeas.name: Complex modulation shape (dual LFO mixer)
patchIdeas.patch: Combine a slow and a fast LFO into one ever-shifting modulation signal, with independent level + polarity on each.
steps: Press CH 1 Cycle; set Rise and Fall for a slow cycle (4–8s total).
Press CH 4 Cycle; set Rise and Fall for a fast cycle (0.5–1s total).
Set CH 2 attenuverter to ~1 o'clock (positive, moderate).
Set CH 3 attenuverter to ~11 o'clock (slight negative).
Patch CH 1 Variable Out 1 → CH 2 Signal In. (This also removes CH 1 from SUM, so SUM only carries what CH 2/3/4 contribute.)
Patch CH 4 Variable Out 4 → CH 3 Signal In. (Same — removes CH 4 from SUM.)
Patch SUM Out → your modulation target (Filter FM, Wavefolder CV, anything).
patchIdeas.signalOut: SUM Out — slow LFO + fast LFO, each scaled and signed by their respective attenuverters.
patchIdeas.hear: Modulation that lopes slowly but jitters on short timescales. Adjust the attenuverters to rebalance — CH 2 dominates = slow character wins, CH 3 dominates = fast chatter wins.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, slow (4-8s)
* CH 4: Cycle ON, fast (0.5-1s)
* CH 2 Atten: ~1 o'clock
* CH 3 Atten: ~11 o'clock

- MATHS.CH 1 (Variable Out 1) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 4 (Variable Out 4) >> MATHS.CH 3 (In)
- MATHS (SUM) >> Filter (FM) // combined dual-LFO shape

patchIdeas.name: Analog clock divider
patchIdeas.patch: Abuse MATHS's retrigger-blocking to turn one fast clock into multiple slower ones — one channel per division.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise to a length longer than your clock's period. MATHS ignores triggers arriving during Rise, so you get half-speed.
Patch your clock → CH 1 Trigger.
Patch CH 1 EOR → destination as a /2 clock.
For further division: set CH 4 Rise longer than CH 1's envelope period.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → CH 4 Trigger.
Patch CH 4 EOC → destination as a /4 clock.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 EOR (/2 clock) and CH 4 EOC (/4 clock). Both are 0V/10V gates.
patchIdeas.hear: Two clock lanes derived from one master, each slower than the previous. Change the Rise lengths on the fly to shift the divisions live.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise longer than clock period
* CH 4: Rise longer than CH 1 envelope period

- Clock Source (Clk) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Destination (/2 Clock)
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) g> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) g> Destination (/4 Clock)

patchIdeas.name: Directional portamento (asymmetric glide)
patchIdeas.patch: Slew pitch CV differently depending on direction — e.g. snap up instantly, glide down slowly.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise full CCW (near-instant attack when pitch jumps up).
Set CH 1 Fall to ~2 o'clock (slow descent when pitch drops).
Set Vari-Response (Curve) at noon for a linear glide.
Patch your pitch sequencer → CH 1 Signal In (NOT Trigger — Signal In lets MATHS act as a slew limiter).
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → VCO V/Oct.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 Unity Out — slewed pitch CV, tracks input but asymmetrically in time.
patchIdeas.hear: Notes that rise cleanly but fall with a downward glide (or vice versa if you reverse Rise and Fall). Great for fretless-bass and synth-lead expressiveness.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise CCW (fast up), Fall ~2 o'clock (slow down), Curve noon

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) p> VCO (V/Oct) // snaps up, glides down

patchIdeas.name: Sub-harmonic generator
patchIdeas.patch: Feed a VCO into CH 4 Trigger at audio rate; CH 4's Cycle running a bit slower than the VCO emits a sub-octave pulse.
steps: Press CH 4 Cycle.
Set CH 4 Rise + Fall very short, but slightly longer than one period of your audio-rate VCO (e.g. a few hundred microseconds).
Patch VCO Out (audio-rate) → CH 4 Trigger. CH 4 tries to retrigger but can't keep up — you get one MATHS cycle per N VCO cycles.
Patch CH 4 EOC → a mixer as your sub-octave pulse.
Mix in the original VCO for a Trautonium-style bass reinforcement.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 4 EOC — pulse train at a sub-octave of the incoming VCO.
patchIdeas.hear: A buzzy square-ish sub-octave below the VCO's pitch. Sweeping Rise/Fall changes the ratio — 2:1 for one octave down, 3:1 for a fifth below, and so on.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Cycle ON, Rise + Fall short (> 1 VCO cycle)

- VCO (Out) -> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig) // audio-rate trigger
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) -> Mixer (In) // sub-octave pulse

patchIdeas.name: Voltage comparator / gate extractor
patchIdeas.patch: Instant Rise + Fall turns CH 1 into a threshold detector — EOR fires a gate whenever the input crosses a level.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise full CCW and Fall full CCW (instant response, no slew).
Set CH 2 attenuverter full CW (unity pass-through). This lets you scale the input up if it's weak.
Patch your slow CV or audio source → CH 2 Signal In.
Patch CH 2 Out → CH 1 Signal In. (Using CH 2 Out means you can trim the signal level; patch directly to CH 1 Signal In if you don't need scaling.)
Patch CH 1 EOR → wherever you want the threshold-crossing gate.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 EOR — 0V/10V gate that fires whenever the scaled input crosses above CH 1's internal threshold.
patchIdeas.hear: A gate every time the source rises past a certain voltage. Useful for extracting rhythmic events from an audio envelope or CV shape.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 2 Atten: full CW
* CH 1: Rise + Fall full CCW (instant)

- CV Source (Out) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Destination (Trig) // gate on threshold crossing

patchIdeas.name: Coupled oscillators (quadrature-triggered pair)
patchIdeas.patch: Get quadrature LFOs without Cycle mode — CH 1 and CH 4 trigger each other forever after a single kick.
steps: Leave CH 1 and CH 4 Cycle OFF.
Set Rise + Fall equal on both channels to whatever period you want.
Patch CH 1 EOR → CH 4 Trigger.
Patch CH 4 EOC → CH 1 Trigger. The pair is now mutually reinforcing.
Briefly press CH 1 Cycle once to kick the pair into motion, then release.
Take CH 1 Unity Out and CH 4 Unity Out as your two CVs — they run 90° apart forever.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 Unity Out and CH 4 Unity Out — two 0–8V LFOs, 90° out of phase.
patchIdeas.hear: Same sonic result as the Quadrature LFOs patch, but without Cycle mode engaged — so they're more easily resettable (cut the EOR→Trig cable to stop, re-kick to restart).
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle OFF (briefly ON to kick off)
* CH 4: Cycle OFF

- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig) // self-sustaining pair
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) >> Destination A (CV)
- MATHS.CH 4 (Unity Out) >> Destination B (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Formant complex oscillator
patchIdeas.patch: Run CH 1 at audio rate with a sharp shape, modulate its Rise with a slow LFO — get a Mangrove-style formant oscillator with a sweeping peak.
steps: Press CH 1 Cycle. Set Rise very short and Fall very short so the cycle runs at audio rate.
Turn Vari-Response (Curve) full CW for a sharp exponential pulse shape.
Press CH 4 Cycle. Set CH 4 Rise + Fall for a slow LFO rate (several seconds total).
Patch CH 4 Unity Out → CH 1 Rise CV. This modulates CH 1's rise time up and down.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → VCA audio input.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 Unity Out — pitched audio at audio rate with a shifting formant shape.
patchIdeas.hear: A voice-like tone whose timbre sweeps between a bright pulse and a softer rounded peak. Scale CH 4's modulation via its attenuverter (CH 4 Atten centered position) if the depth is too wild.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, audio rate, Rise short, Curve full CW
* CH 4: Cycle ON, slow LFO

- MATHS.CH 4 (Unity Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Rise CV) // sweeps formant
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-modulating cycle (audio-rate FM)
patchIdeas.patch: Feed CH 1's audio-rate cycle back into its own Rise CV through CH 2 — produces chaotic pitched FM.
steps: Press CH 1 Cycle. Set Rise short enough to hit audio rate.
Set CH 2 attenuverter between 1 and 2 o'clock (start moderate; this is your chaos knob).
Set CH 1 and CH 3/4 attenuverters to noon so only CH 2 contributes to SUM.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → CH 2 Signal In.
Patch SUM Out → CH 1 Rise CV. The channel now modulates its own rise time from its own output.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out (a second cable from the same jack is fine here since Unity Out isn't normalled) → VCA audio input.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 Unity Out — audio-rate, pitched, with self-FM character.
patchIdeas.hear: A buzzy FM-like tone. CH 2 attenuverter tilts the character — CCW stable and clean, past noon bright and chaotic. Tame too much screech by pulling CH 2 attenuverter back toward noon.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON, audio rate
* CH 2 Atten: ~1-2 o'clock

- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS (SUM) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Rise CV) // self-FM feedback
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Krell patch (self-evolving random)
patchIdeas.patch: Todd Barton's classic: each envelope cycle samples a new random voltage, which sets the next cycle's timing. Envelope durations wander autonomously.
steps: Press CH 4 Cycle — the channel will free-run.
Patch noise → external S&H audio input.
Patch CH 4 EOC → external S&H trigger input. Every time CH 4 completes a cycle, S&H grabs a new random voltage.
Patch S&H Out → CH 4 Rise CV. Now each new random voltage sets the next rise time.
Patch CH 4 Unity Out → VCA CV.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 4 Unity Out — random-length envelopes, 0–8V.
patchIdeas.hear: A stream of envelopes whose lengths keep shifting. No two are the same. Swap S&H → Rise for S&H → Fall for a different flavor; patch both for full randomness.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Cycle ON

- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) t> S&H (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H (In)
- S&H (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Rise CV) // random rise time each cycle
- MATHS.CH 4 (Unity Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Trigger-to-gate converter (VC pulse width)
patchIdeas.patch: Short trigger in, shaped gate out — with independent voltage control over delay and width.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise to the delay you want between incoming trigger and the gate starting.
Set CH 1 Fall to the output gate's pulse width.
Patch your trigger source → CH 1 Trigger.
Patch CH 1 EOR → destination that expects a gate. EOR goes high at end of Rise and stays high through Fall.
Optional: patch a CV → CH 1 Rise CV for voltage-controlled delay, or CH 1 Fall CV for voltage-controlled pulse width.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 EOR — 0V/10V gate, delayed by Rise, width = Fall.
patchIdeas.hear: Take any click or trigger, come out with a properly-shaped, timed gate. Useful for swing, retriggering envelope tails, sample-playback windows.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise = delay, Fall = gate width

- Trigger Source (Trig) t> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Destination (Gate) // delayed gate of Fall width

patchIdeas.name: Bipolar VCA (attenuverter trick)
patchIdeas.patch: Use CH 2 (or CH 3) as a manual-control bipolar VCA — no envelope needed, the attenuverter is the level knob.
steps: Patch your signal source → CH 2 Signal In.
Set CH 2 attenuverter: full CW = unity pass, noon = silence, full CCW = inverted pass. Between these is scaled (CW) or scaled-and-inverted (CCW).
Patch CH 2 Out → your destination.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 2 Out — input scaled and optionally inverted by the attenuverter setting.
patchIdeas.hear: Manual volume control over any signal, with the ability to flip polarity. CH 3 works identically but has a different default offset when unpatched. Great for taming an LFO's depth or inverting a CV without needing a dedicated attenuverter.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 2 Atten: level (CCW invert, CW unity)

- Signal Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) -> Destination (In) // bipolar scaled/inverted copy

patchIdeas.name: Voltage-controlled pulse delay
patchIdeas.patch: Voltage-controlled delay on any trigger — shift a clock lane by a CV-amount for swing, phase offsets, generative timing.
steps: Patch your clock (or any trigger source) → CH 1 Trigger.
Patch a CV source (LFO, sequencer, random) → CH 1 Rise CV. Positive voltage lengthens Rise, negative shortens it.
Patch CH 1 EOR → destination. EOR goes high at the end of Rise — that's your delayed trigger.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 EOR — delayed gate, timing controlled by Rise CV.
patchIdeas.hear: Swing or lag applied to any clock lane. Modulate Rise CV with an LFO for drifting time, with a random CV for generative rhythm.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise CV = delay amount

- Clock Source (Clk) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- CV Source (Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Rise CV)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> Destination (Clk) // delayed by CV

patchIdeas.name: Full-wave rectifier (absolute value)
patchIdeas.patch: Two channels + OR = |signal|. Pass the positive half through one channel, invert and pass the negative half through the other, max them together.
steps: Set CH 1 and CH 4: Rise full CCW, Fall full CCW (both channels act as pass-through for Signal In).
Set CH 2 attenuverter full CCW — CH 2 Out is an inverted copy of whatever enters CH 2 Signal In.
Patch your bipolar signal → CH 1 Signal In (CH 1 passes only the positive half because Unity Out is 0–8V).
Patch the same bipolar signal → CH 2 Signal In.
Patch CH 2 Out → CH 4 Signal In (CH 4 now receives a flipped copy; it passes only the previously-negative half, now flipped to positive).
Patch OR Out → destination. OR takes the higher of CH 1 and CH 4 at any instant — that's |signal|.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OR Out — positive-only rectified version of the bipolar input.
patchIdeas.hear: A signal that's been flipped to always-positive. Doubles perceived rate for any symmetric wave and makes a useful envelope-follower front end.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1 & CH 4: Rise + Fall full CCW (pass-through)
* CH 2 Atten: full CCW (invert)

- Signal Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- Signal Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) -> MATHS.CH 4 (Signal In) // inverted copy
- MATHS (OR) -> Destination (In) // |signal|

patchIdeas.name: Pseudo-VCA with soft clip
patchIdeas.patch: No VCA available? SUM an audio signal with a CV and let the SUM bus rails clip — a gritty AM-style VCA substitute.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise full CCW + Fall full CCW (pass-through for audio at CH 1 Signal In).
Set CH 1 attenuverter at noon (so CH 1 itself doesn't contribute a raw copy to SUM — the signal goes through as a consequence of being at the channel).
Set CH 2 attenuverter somewhere between noon and full CW — this sets AM depth.
Patch your audio source → CH 1 Signal In.
Patch your CV source → CH 2 Signal In.
Patch SUM Out → destination. SUM adds audio and CV, clipping softly at the ±10V rails when the CV swing pushes it there.
patchIdeas.signalOut: SUM Out — audio gated/scaled by the CV at CH 2, with soft clipping.
patchIdeas.hear: Grittier than a clean VCA — the CV doesn't multiply, it adds, and the output saturates when the sum exceeds rails. Good for lo-fi character.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise + Fall full CCW (pass-through)
* CH 2 Atten: sets AM depth

- Audio Source (Out) -> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- CV Source (Out) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS (SUM) -> Destination (In) // AM-scaled with soft clip

patchIdeas.name: Stepped random modulation (no S&H module)
patchIdeas.patch: Turn CH 4 into a sample-and-hold without needing a dedicated S&H module — full CCW Rise/Fall means CH 4 tracks its Signal In only during trigger moments.
steps: Set CH 4 Rise full CCW, Fall full CCW (instant tracking, instant settle — effectively sample-on-trigger).
Patch your clock → CH 1 Trigger.
Patch CH 1 EOR → CH 4 Trigger (so CH 4 samples once per clock).
Patch noise (or any slow-changing CV) → CH 4 Signal In.
Patch CH 4 Unity Out → your modulation destination.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 4 Unity Out — stepped random CV that updates on every clock.
patchIdeas.hear: A sample-and-held voltage that changes each clock step. Use as pitch CV via a quantizer, or as a filter cutoff mod for chaotic bit-crush feel.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Rise + Fall full CCW (sample-on-trig)

- Clock Source (Clk) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (EOR) g> MATHS.CH 4 (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 4 (Unity Out) >> Destination (CV) // stepped random

patchIdeas.name: Triple cross-modulated LFO ring
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 and CH 4 cycle at different rates, feed each other through CH 2 and CH 3, and the SUM modulates CH 1's Rise — an ever-shifting complex LFO.
steps: Press CH 1 Cycle; set Rise + Fall for a medium LFO rate.
Press CH 4 Cycle; set Rise + Fall for a noticeably different rate (~2×).
Set CH 2 and CH 3 attenuverters to moderate positions (~1-2 o'clock).
Patch CH 1 Variable Out 1 → CH 2 Signal In.
Patch CH 4 Variable Out 4 → CH 3 Signal In.
Patch SUM Out → CH 1 Rise CV. This feeds the mixed LFO state back into CH 1's timing.
Patch CH 4 Unity Out → CH 1 Fall CV. Direct influence from CH 4 onto CH 1's fall timing.
Take SUM Out (or CH 1 Unity Out) → your destination.
patchIdeas.signalOut: SUM Out — complex evolving modulation; or CH 1 Unity Out for just CH 1's voice.
patchIdeas.hear: A modulation signal that never repeats the same shape twice. The CH 2 and CH 3 attenuverters are your two personality knobs — turn them to reshape the behavior, from almost-stable to wild lockup territory.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON
* CH 4: Cycle ON (different rate)
* CH 2 & CH 3 Atten: moderate

- MATHS.CH 1 (Variable Out 1) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 4 (Variable Out 4) >> MATHS.CH 3 (In)
- MATHS (SUM) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Rise CV) // cross-mod
- MATHS.CH 4 (Unity Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Fall CV)

patchIdeas.name: Ratchet / burst generator
patchIdeas.patch: Window CH 4's Cycle with a short envelope from CH 1 — each incoming gate produces a burst of accelerating pulses.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise and Fall short (AD envelope, ~100-500ms total length).
Press CH 4 Cycle — CH 4 runs free.
Patch your gate/clock source → CH 1 Trigger.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → CH 4 Rise CV. When CH 1 fires, its envelope controls CH 4's speed — starts fast at the peak, slows as CH 1 fades.
Patch CH 4 EOC → your drum VCA envelope's trigger. Each CH 4 cycle fires a hit.
Silence CH 4 between bursts by ensuring CH 4 Rise (with CV at 0V) is too long to cycle on its own.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 4 EOC — trigger stream with rachet bursts, one burst per incoming gate.
patchIdeas.hear: A burst of hits per trigger, accelerating at the onset and trailing off as CH 1's envelope fades. Perfect for generative drum fills, snare rolls, hihat flurries.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise short, Fall short (AD)
* CH 4: Cycle ON

- Gate Source (Gate) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Rise CV) // windows + accelerates CH 4
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOC) t> VCA Env (Trig) // ratchet bursts

patchIdeas.name: Classic Krell with dual random CVs
patchIdeas.patch: Todd Barton's Krell pattern extended: MATHS + two external S&H channels give you random attack, random decay, AND random pitch on every note.
steps: Press CH 4 Cycle.
Patch noise → S&H 1 Input.
Patch noise → S&H 2 Input.
Patch CH 4 EOR → S&H 1 Trigger.
Patch CH 4 EOR → S&H 2 Trigger (both S&Hs sample fresh values each cycle).
Patch S&H 1 Out → CH 4 Rise CV (randomizes the attack time of each cycle).
Patch S&H 2 Out → CH 4 Fall CV (randomizes the decay time).
Patch CH 4 Unity Out → Quantizer input.
Patch Quantizer Out → VCO V/Oct (pitch follows the envelope's contour, snapped to scale).
Patch CH 4 Unity Out → VCA CV to gate the voice.
Patch VCO → VCA → your output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA audio output — self-evolving melody.
patchIdeas.hear: A never-repeating phrase where every note has its own attack length, decay length, and pitch. Scale the quantizer to taste. The whole patch self-sustains — no external clock needed.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 4: Cycle ON

- MATHS.CH 4 (EOR) t> S&H 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 4 (EOR) t> S&H 2 (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H 1 (In)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H 2 (In)
- S&H 1 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Rise CV) // random attack each cycle
- S&H 2 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Fall CV) // random decay each cycle
- MATHS.CH 4 (Unity Out) >> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- MATHS.CH 4 (Unity Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Buchla low-pass-gate bongo
patchIdeas.patch: Use MATHS's exponential fall as a pluck/LPG contour — get Buchla-style plucked percussion without a dedicated LPG.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise very short.
Set CH 1 Fall to medium (a few hundred ms).
Turn Vari-Response (Curve) full CW for an exponential fall — the plucked character comes from this shape.
Patch your clock → CH 1 Trigger.
Patch random CV → VCO V/Oct (or a sequencer for melody).
Patch VCO Out → VCA audio input.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → VCA CV. This envelope shapes the VCA like a Buchla LPG would — sharp attack, expo release.
Patch VCA Out → your output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA Out — plucked percussive tones.
patchIdeas.hear: Bongo/bell-like percussive hits with natural decay. Random pitch + exponential MATHS envelope = DPO-plus-Optomix character in a pinch. Tighten Fall for shorter plucks, extend for bells.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise very short, Fall medium, Curve full CW (expo)

- Clock (Out) c> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- Random CV (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) >> VCA (CV) // LPG-style plucked contour
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In) // Buchla bongo

patchIdeas.name: Self-patched Both (CH 1 ↔ CH 4 cross-mod)
patchIdeas.patch: The illustrated 'Both' patch from the MATHS manual — CH 1 and CH 4 reshape each other's timing, producing warping complex LFO shapes.
steps: Press CH 1 Cycle and CH 4 Cycle (both free-run).
Set CH 2 and CH 3 attenuverters to moderate settings (~1-2 o'clock) — these tame the cross-modulation depth.
Patch CH 1 Variable Out 1 → CH 3 Signal In.
Patch CH 3 Out → CH 4 Rise CV. CH 1's envelope now warps CH 4's rise timing.
Patch CH 4 Variable Out 4 → CH 2 Signal In.
Patch CH 2 Out → CH 1 Fall CV. CH 4's envelope warps CH 1's fall timing.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out (or CH 4 Unity Out) → your mod destination. For chaotic melody, patch via a quantizer to VCO V/Oct.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CH 1 Unity Out or CH 4 Unity Out — two complex LFOs that shape each other's timing.
patchIdeas.hear: Two related LFOs that constantly warp and reshape each other. Beautiful for evolving pads, chaotic sequencing, or generative modulation streams. Start with attenuverters low for gentle interaction, increase for full lockup territory.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Cycle ON
* CH 4: Cycle ON
* CH 2 & CH 3 Atten: moderate

- MATHS.CH 1 (Variable Out 1) >> MATHS.CH 3 (In)
- MATHS.CH 3 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 4 (Rise CV) // CH 1 warps CH 4
- MATHS.CH 4 (Variable Out 4) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> MATHS.CH 1 (Fall CV) // CH 4 warps CH 1
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) p> VCO (V/Oct) // chaotic pitch drift

patchIdeas.name: Slew + sum + inverter (three jobs, one module)
patchIdeas.patch: Use MATHS as a utility swiss-army knife — portamento on one channel, inversion on another, CV summing on a third. One module, three jobs for a whole voice.
steps: Set CH 1 Rise and Fall both short (portamento slew — the slew time is shared by both directions).
Set CH 2 attenuverter full CCW (CH 2 Out will be an inverted copy of its input).
Set CH 3 attenuverter at ~1 o'clock (positive scaling for mod mixing).
Patch your sequencer pitch → CH 1 Signal In.
Patch CH 1 Unity Out → VCO V/Oct (pitch with portamento).
Patch an LFO → CH 2 Signal In.
Patch CH 2 Out → Filter Cutoff (inverted LFO).
Patch a second modulator → CH 3 Signal In.
Patch SUM Out → VCA CV. SUM carries CH 2 + CH 3 combined.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Three outputs: CH 1 Unity Out (slewed pitch), CH 2 Out (inverted LFO), SUM Out (mixed mod).
patchIdeas.hear: One MATHS doing three utility jobs for a single voice — glide on pitch, inverted modulation on filter, and mixed modulation on amplitude. Efficient use of HP when you don't have dedicated slew/inverter/mixer modules.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise short, Fall short (slew)
* CH 2 Atten: full CCW (invert)
* CH 3 Atten: ~1 o'clock

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> MATHS.CH 1 (Signal In)
- MATHS.CH 1 (Unity Out) p> VCO (V/Oct) // slewed pitch
- LFO (Out) >> MATHS.CH 2 (In)
- MATHS.CH 2 (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff) // inverted LFO
- Mod A (Out) >> MATHS.CH 3 (In)
- MATHS (SUM) >> VCA (CV) // CH 2+CH 3 summed
